title: Confirm Lazy-Loading Behavior for Multi-Org Object Fields
description:
## Context

Verification that the multi-org feature does not automatically download all object fields when a second Salesforce instance is connected. This ensures efficient API usage and avoids unnecessary data transfer.

## Confirmed Behavior

- On authentication of a second instance, only the list of objects is fetched — not fields for every object.
- Object fields are fetched on demand when a user clicks on a specific object to view its details.
- A refresh/sync option is available for users to re-fetch fields from Salesforce if changes have been made on the Salesforce side.

This lazy-loading approach keeps initial connection fast and avoids hitting Salesforce API limits by deferring field metadata retrieval until the user actually needs it.
acceptance_criteria: On initial instance connection, only the object list is fetched — individual object fields are not downloaded automatically
Object fields are fetched on demand when the user clicks into an object's detail view
A refresh/sync button is available to re-fetch fields from Salesforce for a given object
No unnecessary Salesforce API calls are made during the initial connection process
implementation_notes: No code changes — this ticket documents confirmed behavior from the MBT-932 implementation. Fields are lazy-loaded per object on user click, not bulk-fetched on instance connection. A sync/refresh option exists for re-fetching fields after Salesforce-side changes.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed that fields are not fetched automatically on authentication. Only the object list is retrieved initially. Fields are fetched on demand when the user views an object, and a refresh option is provided to sync with Salesforce changes.
